scenario_description: A child in a blue-and-white striped shirt moves around a park bench while other people and objects appear and disappear, requiring the viewer to track the child's position relative to static background elements across multiple turns.

turns.text: Here is the first shot. The kid looks bored.
turns.image_description: A medium shot of a wooden park bench against a brick wall. On the left side of the bench sits a young child wearing a blue-and-white horizontal striped t-shirt and denim shorts. The child is facing the camera, legs swinging back and forth. To the right of the child, on the same bench, sits a red plastic bucket. In the background, slightly out of focus, a tall oak tree stands behind the bench.

turns.text: Yeah, they look pretty restless. Is that red bucket theirs?
They got up and moved over there.
turns.image_description: The same wooden bench and brick wall. The child in the blue-and-white striped shirt is now standing at the far right end of the bench, near where the red bucket was previously sitting. The red bucket is now on the ground next to the bench leg. The child is facing away from the camera, looking towards the right edge of the frame. A second child, wearing a yellow raincoat, is walking into the frame from the left, passing behind the bench.

turns.text: Okay, so they swapped spots with the bucket? And who is that in yellow?
Just a crowd passing by. Can you still see the one in stripes?
turns.image_description: The view of the bench is partially obstructed. A large group of adults in dark coats walks rapidly from left to right directly in front of the bench, blocking most of the view. Through a small gap between two adults, only the lower half of the child in the blue-and-white striped shirt is visible; they are now sitting on the far right end of the bench, facing away from the camera (back to the viewer). The red bucket is visible on the ground near their feet. The child in the yellow raincoat is no longer in the frame.

turns.text: Barely! I can just catch a glimpse of the striped pants through the gap. They seem to have sat down again.

turns.image_description: The bench is fully visible again. The child in the blue-and-white striped shirt is sitting on the far right end of the bench, facing completely away from the camera, obscuring their face. Their posture is slumped. The red bucket is still on the ground nearby. The child in the yellow raincoat has re-entered the scene and is now sitting on the far left end of the bench, facing the camera, smiling.

turns.text: Oh, interesting. Now there are two kids. The one in yellow is looking right at us, but the one in stripes is hiding their face.
Wait, which one is the original kid? A) The one in the yellow raincoat B) The one in the blue-and-white striped shirt C) Neither, both are new D) The one holding the red bucket

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: Turn 1 establishes the 'original kid' as the child in the blue-and-white striped shirt sitting on the left. Turn 3 shows this same child moving to the right side of the bench. Turn 5 confirms the child in stripes is still present despite the obstruction. Turn 7 shows the child in stripes sitting on the right with their back turned, while a NEW child in a yellow raincoat appears on the left. Therefore, the original entity is the one in the striped shirt (Option B). Option A is incorrect because the yellow raincoat child did not exist in Turn 1. Option C is incorrect because the striped child is clearly tracked across all turns. Option D is incorrect because neither child is currently holding the bucket (it is on the ground).
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The model must track the specific visual attribute (blue-and-white striped shirt) of the target entity across three distinct spatial configurations (left-facing, right-standing, right-back-turned) while filtering out a distractor entity (yellow raincoat) that enters later and occupies the original starting position (left side), potentially confusing a model relying solely on spatial memory or the final image state.

_meta.scenario: A child in a striped shirt sits swinging their legs; they eventually stand up, walk to the other end of the bench, and sit facing away from the camera, obscuring their face. Later, a large group walks in front of the bench, briefly blocking the view entirely before revealing the child again.
